solution:
Aromatic aldehydes do not respond Fehling's test. (A), (B), (C) are aromatic aldehydes.

If N and S attached with carbon atom give blood red with sodium nitroprusside gives a blood red colour but not prussian blue.

solution:
(A) Bayer's test $\rightarrow$ Unsaturation
(B) Ceric ammonium nitrate test $\rightarrow$ Alcoholic-OH group
(C) Phthalein dye test $\rightarrow$ Phenol
(D) Schiff's test $\rightarrow$ Aldehyde

question:
Consider the following reaction

Propanal+Methanal=→(ii) Δ(iii) NaCN(iv) H3O+(i) dil. NaOHProduct B(C5H8O3)

racemic mixture and gives a gas with saturated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NaHCO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> solution
optically active alcohol and is neutrall
solution:
Propanal and methanal undergo aldol condensation reaction with sodium hydroxide. The α,β-unsaturated compound undergo 1,4-conjugated addition with cyanide ion gives a racemic mixture. The cyanide undergo acidic hydrolysis gives carboxylic acid.

Carboxylic acid will give CO2 gas, with NaHCO3 solution

subject: Chemistry
q: '$x$' is the product which is obtained from benzene by reacting it with carbon monoxide and hydrogen chloride in the presence of cuprous chloride. '$y$' is the major product obtained from the benzene by reacting it with ethanoyl chloride in the presence of anhydrous AlCl$_3$. Product (major) obtained by heating $x$ and $y$ in the presence of alkali is $z$. Total number of $\pi$ (pi) electrons in $z$ is ________.
question: '$x$' is the product which is obtained from benzene by reacting it with carbon monoxide and hydrogen chloride in the presence of cuprous chloride. '$y$' is the major product obtained from the benzene by reacting it with ethanoyl chloride in the presence of anhydrous AlCl$_3$. Product (major) obtained by heating $x$ and $y$ in the presence of alkali is $z$. Total number of $\pi$ (pi) electrons in $z$ is ________.
solution:
Benzene reacts with $CO$ and $HCl$ in the presence of $CuCl$ to give benzaldehyde ($x$) via the Gattermann-Koch reaction.

Benzene reacts with ethanoyl chloride ($CH_3COCl$) in the presence of anhydrous $AlCl_3$ to give acetophenone ($y$) via Friedel-Crafts acylation.

Heating benzaldehyde ($x$) and acetophenone ($y$) in the presence of alkali results in a crossed aldol condensation to form benzalacetophenone ($z$).

$C_6H_5CHO + CH_3COC_6H_5 \xrightarrow{OH^-, \Delta} C_6H_5-CH=CH-CO-C_6H_5 + H_2O$

The structure of $z$ ($C_6H_5-CH=CH-CO-C_6H_5$) contains two phenyl rings ($6$ $\pi$ bonds), one $C=C$ double bond ($1$ $\pi$ bond), and one $C=O$ double bond ($1$ $\pi$ bond).

Total number of $\pi$ bonds = $6 + 1 + 1 = 8$

Total number of $\pi$ electrons = $8 \times 2 = 16$

Answer: $16$

subject: Chemistry
q: This reduction reaction is known as:
question: This reduction reaction is known as:
solution:
The Rosenmund reaction is a hydrogenation process where molecular hydrogen reacts with the acyl chloride in the presence of a catalyst – palladium on barium sulphate. Barium sulphate reduces the activity of palladium due to its low surface area, thereby preventing over reduction.

It is known as rosenmund reduction, which is the partial reduction of acid chloride to aldehyde.

Hence, the answer is option A.

q: In Tollen's test for aldehyde, the overall number of electron(s) transferred to the Tollen's reagent formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Ag</mi><msub><mfenced><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo></msup></math> per aldehyde group to form silver mirror is ___.(Round off to the Nearest integer)
question: In Tollen's test for aldehyde, the overall number of electron(s) transferred to the Tollen's reagent formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Ag</mi><msub><mfenced><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo></msup></math> per aldehyde group to form silver mirror is ___.(Round off to the Nearest integer)
solution:
AgNO3+NaOH→AgOH+NaNO3

2AgOH→Ag2O+H2O

Ag2O+4NH3+H2O→2AgNH32++2O⊝H

Tollens' reagent is an alkaline solution of ammoniacal silver nitrate and is used to test for aldehydes. Tollens' test, also known as silver-mirror test, is a qualitative laboratory test used to distinguish between an aldehyde and a ketone. It exploits the fact that aldehydes are readily oxidized (see oxidation), whereas ketones are not.

subject: Chemistry
q: '$x$' is the product which is obtained by the hydrolysis of prop-1-yne in the presence of mercuric sulphate under dilute acidic medium at $333$ K. '$y$' is the product which is obtained by the reaction of ethane nitrile with methyl magnesium bromide in dry ether followed by hydrolysis. IUPAC name of product obtained from '$x$' and '$y$' in the presence of barium hydroxide followed by heating is :
question: '$x$' is the product which is obtained by the hydrolysis of prop-1-yne in the presence of mercuric sulphate under dilute acidic medium at $333$ K. '$y$' is the product which is obtained by the reaction of ethane nitrile with methyl magnesium bromide in dry ether followed by hydrolysis. IUPAC name of product obtained from '$x$' and '$y$' in the presence of barium hydroxide followed by heating is :
solution:
Hydrolysis of prop-1-yne in the presence of $HgSO_4$ and dilute $H_2SO_4$ yields acetone.

$CH_3-C \equiv CH + H_2O \xrightarrow{Hg^{2+}, H^+} CH_3-CO-CH_3$

Thus, $x$ is acetone.

Reaction of ethane nitrile ($CH_3CN$) with methyl magnesium bromide ($CH_3MgBr$) followed by hydrolysis also yields acetone.

$CH_3-C \equiv N + CH_3MgBr \rightarrow CH_3-C(CH_3)=NMgBr \xrightarrow{H_3O^+} CH_3-CO-CH_3$

Thus, $y$ is acetone.

Reaction of $x$ and $y$ (both acetone) in the presence of $Ba(OH)_2$ followed by heating results in an aldol condensation.

$2 CH_3-CO-CH_3 \xrightarrow{Ba(OH)_2} CH_3-C(OH)(CH_3)-CH_2-CO-CH_3$

Heating causes dehydration to form an unsaturated ketone.

$CH_3-C(OH)(CH_3)-CH_2-CO-CH_3 \xrightarrow{\Delta} CH_3-C(CH_3)=CH-CO-CH_3$

Numbering the principal carbon chain to give the lowest locant to the ketone group, the IUPAC name of the product is 4-methylpent-3-en-2-one.

Answer: 4-Methylpent-3-en-2-one
